body:
Title: CRA 2026 provincial and territorial income tax rates.

Sources:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/tax/individuals/tax-rates-brackets/current-year.html
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/payroll/t4127-payroll-deductions-formulas/t4127-jan/t4127-jan-payroll-deductions-formulas-computer-programs.html
- https://www.revenuquebec.ca/en/citizens/income-tax-return/completing-your-income-tax-return/income-tax-rates/

CRA current year tax rates and income brackets (2026):
For income earned in 2026, applied to the 2026 tax return.
Provincial or territorial tax rates apply based on province or territory of residence on December 31, 2026.

Alberta rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 61200 at 8% (0.08); 61200.01 to 154259 at 10% (0.10); 154259.01 to 185111 at 12% (0.12); 185111.01 to 246813 at 13% (0.13); 246813.01 to 370220 at 14% (0.14); 370220.01 and above at 15% (0.15).

British Columbia rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 50363 at 5.6% (0.056); 50363.01 to 100728 at 7.7% (0.077); 100728.01 to 115648 at 10.5% (0.105); 115648.01 to 140430 at 12.29% (0.1229); 140430.01 to 190405 at 14.7% (0.147); 190405.01 to 265545 at 16.8% (0.168); 265545.01 and above at 20.5% (0.205).

Manitoba rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 47564 at 10.8% (0.108); 47564.01 to 101200 at 12.75% (0.1275); 101200.01 and above at 17.4% (0.174).

New Brunswick rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 52333 at 9.4% (0.094); 52333.01 to 104666 at 14% (0.14); 104666.01 to 193861 at 16% (0.16); 193861.01 and above at 19.5% (0.195).

Newfoundland and Labrador rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 44678 at 8.7% (0.087); 44678.01 to 89354 at 14.5% (0.145); 89354.01 to 159528 at 15.8% (0.158); 159528.01 to 223340 at 17.8% (0.178); 223340.01 to 285319 at 19.8% (0.198); 285319.01 to 570638 at 20.8% (0.208); 570638.01 to 1141275 at 21.3% (0.213); 1141275.01 and above at 21.8% (0.218).

Northwest Territories rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 53003 at 5.9% (0.059); 53003.01 to 106009 at 8.6% (0.086); 106009.01 to 172346 at 12.2% (0.122); 172346.01 and above at 14.05% (0.1405).

Nova Scotia rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 30995 at 8.79% (0.0879); 30995.01 to 61991 at 14.95% (0.1495); 61991.01 to 97417 at 16.67% (0.1667); 97417.01 to 157124 at 17.5% (0.175); 157124.01 and above at 21% (0.21).

Nunavut rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 55801 at 4% (0.04); 55801.01 to 111602 at 7% (0.07); 111602.01 to 181439 at 9% (0.09); 181439.01 and above at 11.5% (0.115).

Ontario rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 53891 at 5.05% (0.0505); 53891.01 to 107785 at 9.15% (0.0915); 107785.01 to 150000 at 11.16% (0.1116); 150000.01 to 220000 at 12.16% (0.1216); 220000.01 and above at 13.16% (0.1316).

Prince Edward Island rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 33928 at 9.5% (0.095); 33928.01 to 65820 at 13.47% (0.1347); 65820.01 to 106890 at 16.6% (0.166); 106890.01 to 142520 at 17.62% (0.1762); 142520.01 to 200000 at 19% (0.19); 200000.01 and above at 20% (0.20).

Quebec 2026 income tax rates from Revenu Quebec: taxable income 54345 or less at 14% (0.14); more than 54345 but not more than 108680 at 19% (0.19); more than 108680 but not more than 132245 at 24% (0.24); more than 132245 at 25.75% (0.2575).

Saskatchewan rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 54532 at 10.5% (0.105); 54532.01 to 155805 at 12.5% (0.125); 155805.01 and above at 14.5% (0.145).

Yukon rate: 2026. Taxable income brackets and rates: 0 to 58523 at 6.4% (0.064); 58523.01 to 117045 at 9% (0.09); 117045.01 to 181440 at 10.9% (0.109); 181440.01 to 500000 at 12.8% (0.128); 500000.01 and above at 15% (0.15).

CRA T4127 Payroll Deductions Formulas, 122nd edition, effective January 1, 2026:
Step 4 states that annual basic provincial or territorial tax T4 equals (V x A) - KP - K1P - K2P - K3P - K4P - K5P. If the result is negative, T4 equals 0.
Step 5 states that annual provincial or territorial tax deduction T2 equals T4 + V1 + V2 - S - (P x LCP). If the result is negative, T2 equals 0. For employees in Quebec, T2 equals 0.
K1P equals lowest provincial tax rate times TCP. K2P is the provincial or territorial CPP/EI credit. K3P is other provincial or territorial non-refundable tax credits. Unless specified, K4P, LCP, S, V1, and V2 equal 0.
Table 8.2 gives 2026 basic amounts: Alberta 22769; British Columbia 13216; New Brunswick 13664; Newfoundland and Labrador 11188; Nova Scotia 11932; Northwest Territories 18198; Nunavut 19659; Ontario 12989; Prince Edward Island 15000; Saskatchewan 20381.
Manitoba maximum BPAMB is 15780.
Yukon maximum BPAYT is 16452.
Alberta K5P supplemental non-refundable tax credit equals ((K1P + K2P) - 4896.00) x 0.25, floored at 0.
British Columbia S equals the lesser of T4 and 575 where A is not more than 25570. Where A is more than 25570 and not more than 41722, S equals the lesser of T4 and 575 - ((A - 25570) x 0.0356). Where A is more than 41722, S equals 0.
Manitoba LCP equals the lesser of 1800 and 15% (0.15) of the approved labour-sponsored share amount. New Brunswick LCP equals the lesser of 2000 and 20% (0.20). Nova Scotia LCP equals the lesser of 2000 and 20% (0.20). Saskatchewan LCP equals the lesser of 875 and 17.5% (0.175).
Ontario V1 surtax equals 0 where T4 is not more than 5818, equals 0.20 x (T4 - 5818) where T4 is more than 5818 and not more than 7446, and equals (0.20 x (T4 - 5818)) + (0.36 x (T4 - 7446)) where T4 is more than 7446.
Ontario V2 health premium equals 0 where A is not more than 20000. Where A is more than 20000 and not more than 36000, V2 is the lesser of 300 and 0.06 x (A - 20000). Where A is more than 36000 and not more than 48000, V2 is the lesser of 450 and 300 + 0.06 x (A - 36000). Where A is more than 48000 and not more than 72000, V2 is the lesser of 600 and 450 + 0.25 x (A - 48000). Where A is more than 72000 and not more than 200000, V2 is the lesser of 750 and 600 + 0.25 x (A - 72000). Where A is more than 200000, V2 is the lesser of 900 and 750 + 0.25 x (A - 200000).
Ontario S reduction is the lesser of T4 + V1 and (2 x (300 + Y)) - (T4 + V1), floored at 0.
Yukon K4P is the lesser of 0.064 x A, where A is annual gross employment income before deductions, and 0.064 x CEA. Table 8.2 gives Yukon CEA as 1501.
